title: Why is the frontalis harder to treat？ The main reason is the constant balancing act, I explain more
filename: Why is the frontalis harder to treat？ The main reason is the constant balancing act, I explain more.en.vtt
excerpt: So why is the frontalis harder than other areas if you think about it it's one of the few areas where we're constantly trying to balance relaxed and unrelaxed muscle this is the biggest thing about it is that we need to leave the lower area of the frontalis active and in females …

segments.text: so why is the frontalis harder than other areas if you think about it it's one of the few areas where we're constantly trying to balance relaxed and unrelaxed muscle this is the biggest thing about it is that we need to leave the lower area of the frontalis active and in females more active if you want a lifting eyebrow and the upper area needs to be treated so that we get some result so there's actually a constant Balancing Act going on and if you treat it too heavily in the middle and too little at the sides you get a spot brow if you treat it too heavily all the way across you get a heaviness to the whole way across the brow you can drop eyebrows very easily you can also under treat and get weird movements and spasms and things that an eyebrow asymmetries in particular are a nightmare for new injectors when you have a lift to the eyebrow one just lifts half a centimeter more which is only a small difference in your injection technique but it all boils down to the same problem which is that we're semi relaxing the muscle and
we're in a constant Balancing Act

keyPoints.text: So why is the frontalis harder than other areas if you think about it it's one of the few areas where we're constantly trying to balance relaxed and unrelaxed muscle this is the biggest thing about it is that we need to …

transcript: so why is the frontalis harder than other areas if you think about it it's one of the few areas where we're constantly trying to balance relaxed and unrelaxed muscle this is the biggest thing about it is that we need to leave the lower area of the frontalis active and in females more active if you want a lifting eyebrow and the upper area needs to be treated so that we get some result so there's actually a constant Balancing Act going on and if you treat it too heavily in the middle and too little at the sides you get a spot brow if you treat it too heavily all the way across you get a heaviness to the whole way across the brow you can drop eyebrows very easily you can also under treat and get weird movements and spasms and things that an eyebrow asymmetries in particular are a nightmare for new injectors when you have a lift to the eyebrow one just lifts half a centimeter more which is only a small difference in your injection technique but it all boils down to the same problem which is that we're semi relaxing the muscle and we're in a constant Balancing Act
